title: Internal fault when constructing sequence of assignment
body:
The following program produces an internal fault when running `verilator --lint-only`:
```verilog
`default_nettype none

module add_one ();
    logic out_write_en;
    logic reg_to_mem_go_in;
    logic reg_to_mem_done_out;
    initial begin
        out_write_en = 1'd0;
        reg_to_mem_go_in = 1'd0;
    end
    assign out_write_en = reg_to_mem_go_in;
    assign reg_to_mem_go_in = ~reg_to_mem_done_out;
endmodule
```
Output:
```
% verilator --lint-only out.sv        
%Error: Verilator internal fault, sorry. Suggest trying --debug --gdbbt
%Error: Command Failed /usr/local/Cellar/verilator/5.002/bin/verilator_bin --lint-only out.sv
```

Version: Verilator 5.002 2022-10-29 rev UNKNOWN.REV
What OS and distribution are you using? MacOS Ventura

Debug mode points to this line:
```
- V3Ast.cpp:1121:     Dumping obj_dir/Vout_026_dfg-optimize.tree
%Error: Internal Error: out.sv:9:9: ../V3Broken.cpp:173: Broken link in node (or something without maybePointedTo): 'm_varp && !m_varp->brokeExists()' @ ../V3AstNodes.cpp:64
                                  : ... In instance add_one
-node: VARREF 0x7f87e070f3b0 <e122> {c9aj} @dt=0x7f87e070d110@(G/w1)  reg_to_mem_go_in [LV] => VAR 0x7f87e070d540 <e183#> {c5al} @dt=0x7f87e070d110@(G/w1)  reg_to_mem_go_in [VSTATIC]  VAR
    9 |         reg_to_mem_go_in = 1'd0;
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```
